original_abstract: This study explores the integration of Islamic Education values into the Strengthening Pancasila Student Profile and Work Culture Project (P5BK) at SMKN 1 Masbagik. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive approach, data were gathered through in-depth interviews, field observations, and documentation reviews, subsequently analyzed using the Miles and Huberman interactive model. The findings reveal that Islamic values are woven into P5BK through three core thematic pillars: sustainable lifestyles, employability, and local wisdom. The implementation follows a structured four-stage process—planning, execution, evaluation, and identifying supportive factors—which infuses professional vocational training with spiritual principles such as environmental stewardship (*khalifah fil ardh*) and Islamic work ethics. Key advantages of this integrated model include its operational flexibility, the creation of strong synergies between the school and the industrial community, and the provision of authentic learning experiences that heighten students' social and environmental consciousness. Beyond practical applications, this research offers theoretical support for the "Independent Learning" (*Kurikulum Merdeka*) framework, suggesting that a holistic, value-based approach can significantly enhance teacher competence and the overall quality of character development in vocational education.
humanized_abstract: This study aimed to find out how the values of Islamic Education can be connected to the Strengthening Pancasila Student Profile and Work Culture Program (P5BK) which is implemented at SMKN 1 Masbagik. The method used in this study is qualitative descriptive. Data collection was carried out by providing deep interviews, carrying out field observations, and examining documents relating to the implementation of P5BK program at SMKN 1 Masbagik. Using Miles and Huberman interactive model, data analysis carried out in this study found out that Islamic values taught to students can be linked through three themes, namely: sustainable living, employability, and local wisdom. Through a four-stage work cycle—planning, doing, evaluating, and the support factor—Islamic Educational values can be applied into Professional Vocational Training such as *khalifah fil ardh* (earth stewardship) and Islamic work ethics. This activity has an advantage, namely adaptability of work activities, making synergy among teachers and the community, as well as providing a real activity that increase students' social and environmental awareness. On the theoretical side, this research is expected to prove that implementation of values of Islamic Education that is embedded in P5BK program in SMKN 1 Masbagik can support the Independent Learning theory for Kurikulum Merdeka. Hence, value-based approaches can improve teacher competency, not only morally but also professionally in developing student characters through vocational training.
